steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Before the blank: the noun phrase Disyllabic words (subject). After the blank: content ending with the main verb consist. The blank position requires a modifier phrase, not a finite verb.

steps.title: Apply the rule for reduced relative clauses
steps.content:
To maintain a single main predicate, use a past participle in a reduced relative clause rather than a finite verb.

The participle classified correctly modifies words, leaving consist as the sole main verb.

Options A, C, and D use finite verbs, creating two uncoordinated predicates.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
